data.content: I'll start `dotnet watch test` which will automatically re-run your tests whenever you save a file change:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to use hot reload to iterate faster on failing tests. In .NET, the way to do this is with `dotnet watch test`, which will re-run tests automatically whenever files change.

Let me set this up for them. I'll start `dotnet watch test` in async mode so they can see it running and iterate.

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-c1a64c36ce304ab3b3f6ad124fc4df9a && dotnet watch test --no-restore 2>&1", "description": "Start dotnet watch test for hot reload", "mode": "async", "shellId": "watch"}

data.reasoningText: It seems like `dotnet watch test` ran and exited quickly. The output shows "Waiting for changes" then "Exited" then "Waiting for a file to change before restarting". It ran the tests but the output isn't showing test results. Let me try again with more verbose output or restore first.

data.content: Now let me start `dotnet watch test` — this gives you hot reload so tests re-run on every file save:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test with hot reload

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test with hot reload

data.reasoningText:
There's a compilation error - `ArgumentException` is not found because there's no `using System;` directive. But the user asked me to set up hot reload, not to fix the tests. Let me just set up `dotnet watch test` properly and let them know what's happening. Actually wait - the test also has a logic bug: the express test expects 15.00m but the code returns 10.00m. So there are two issues:
1. Missing `using System;` (compilation error)
2. Express shipping returns 10 instead of 15

But the user just asked me to set up hot reload. Let me do that. First let me make sure the project can build (the user said they have "failing tests" which suggests they expect the tests to at least compile and run, but fail). Let me just set up `dotnet watch test` properly.


I'll go ahead and start the watch mode so they can see what's currently happening with the tests and iterate from there.

data.content:
`dotnet watch test` is now running with hot reload. It will automatically re-run tests whenever you save a file.

**Current status:** There's a build error — `ShippingCalculatorTests.cs` is missing `using System;` (needed for `ArgumentException` on line 25). Once you fix that, you'll also see the express delivery test failing (expects `15.00m` but returns `10.00m`).

The watcher is active and waiting for file changes. Every time you save `ShippingCalculatorTests.cs`, it will rebuild and re-run the tests automatically. Would you like me to fix the build error so you can start iterating on the test logic?
